words.m: conjunction + verb qal cohortative 1st common plur

verses.flow: The vision (chazon) of Obadiah. Thus says the Lord YHWH concerning Edom: We have heard a report (shemu'ah) from YHWH, and a messenger has been sent among the nations: "Rise up! Let us rise against her for battle!"
verses.note: HEBREW: "Chazon" (H2377, from chazah, "to see/behold in vision") designates a prophetic revelation received in an exalted visionary state. "Shemu'ah" (H8052, "report/rumor/tidings") frames the oracle as a divinely-dispatched intelligence — the nations are being summoned to divine judgment. HISTORICAL: Edom (Esau's descendants, Gen 25:30) occupies the mountainous terrain south and east of the Dead Sea, the region of Seir; their pride in geographic inaccessibility is a recurring prophetic motif (Jer 49:14-16).

words.m: verb qal perfect 1st common sing + suffix

verses.flow: Behold, I have made you small among the nations; you are utterly despised.

words.m: verb hiphil perfect 3rd masc sing + suffix
verb hiphil imperfect 3rd masc sing + suffix

verses.flow: The pride (zadon) of your heart has deceived you — you who dwell in the clefts of the rock, in your lofty habitation; you who say in your heart, "Who will bring me down to the ground?"
verses.note: HEBREW: "Zadon" (H2087, from zud, "to boil/seethe") denotes arrogant presumption arising from inward pride. The phrase "clefts of the rock" (chagvei ha-sela) almost certainly alludes to Petra, the rose-red city carved into Edomite sandstone cliffs, emblematic of Edom's false sense of invulnerability. THEOLOGICAL: Pride (zadon) is consistently identified in wisdom literature as the root of national and individual destruction (Prov 16:18); Edom here becomes a paradigmatic case of hubris brought low.

words.m: verb hiphil imperfect 1st common sing + suffix

verses.flow: "Though you soar aloft like the eagle, though your nest is set among the stars, from there I will bring you down," declares YHWH.
verses.note: THEOLOGICAL: The imagery of ascending "among the stars" may carry deliberate resonance with the fall of the "son of the dawn" (Isa 14:12-15), where Babel's king is likened to a heavenly being who sought to exalt his throne above the stars of El. DIVINE_COUNCIL: "Stars" in ANE cosmology frequently denote divine beings or heavenly council members; Edom's pride thus echoes a cosmic archetype of rebellion against YHWH's sovereign order.

verses.flow: If thieves came to you, if plunderers by night — how you have been destroyed! — would they not steal only what they needed? If grape-gatherers came to you, would they not leave some gleanings?
How Esau has been searched out! How his hidden treasures have been sought out!

words.m: verb piel perfect 3rd common plur + suffix
verb hiphil perfect 3rd common plur + suffix

verses.flow: All the men of your alliance have driven you to the border; the men who were at peace with you have deceived you and overpowered you; those who ate your bread have set a trap beneath you — there is no understanding (binah) in him.
verses.note: HISTORICAL: Edom's "allies" (anshei beritekha, lit. "men of your covenant") and "men of peace" (anshei shlomekha) likely refers to the Arab tribes and neighboring peoples with whom Edom had treaty relationships. Archaeological and textual evidence from the Nabataean period attests to complex webs of alliance in the Transjordanian region. HEBREW: "Binah" (H998, from bin, "to discern/understand") — its absence in Edom signals not merely tactical failure but a divinely-ordained forfeiture of wisdom, paralleling the broader motif of YHWH removing wisdom from those under judgment (v. 8).

words.m: conjunction + verb hiphil sequential perfect 1st common sing

words.g: from a mountain or range of hills

verses.flow: Will I not on that day, declares YHWH, destroy the wise men out of Edom, and understanding (binah) out of the mount of Esau?

words.m: conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 3rd common plur

words.g: from a mountain or range of hills

verses.flow: And your mighty men shall be shattered, O Teman, so that every man from the mount of Esau will be cut off by slaughter.

words.m: verb piel imperfect 3rd fem sing + suffix
conjunction + verb niphal sequential perfect 2nd masc sing

verses.flow: Because of the violence (chamas) done to your brother Jacob, shame shall cover you, and you shall be cut off forever.
verses.note: HEBREW: "Chamas" (H2555, "violence/wrong/cruelty") is a theologically weighted term used of the violence that provoked the Flood (Gen 6:11) and of systemic societal injustice (Hab 1:2-3). Its use here connects Edom's treatment of Judah to a category of sin that YHWH finds particularly grievous. TYPOLOGY: The Jacob-Esau conflict (Gen 25-36) prefigures the perpetual enmity between flesh and spirit, the covenant people and the world system; Edom's betrayal at Jerusalem's fall (586 B.C.) concretizes this typological tension historically.

verses.flow: On the day that you stood aloof, on the day that strangers carried off his wealth and foreigners entered his gates and cast lots (goral) for Jerusalem, you were like one of them.
verses.note: HISTORICAL: "Cast lots (goral) for Jerusalem" almost certainly refers to the Babylonian conquest of Jerusalem in 586 B.C. (cf. 2 Kgs 25; Ps 137:7, where Edom's complicity is explicitly remembered). Edom's passive stance — "you stood aloof" (mi-neged amad) — escalated into active collaboration with the conquerors, making them morally equivalent to the invaders. THEOLOGICAL: Covenantal solidarity between brother-nations (rooted in Abraham's family) was violated; the indictment is not merely political but covenantal.

verses.flow: But you should not have gloated over the day of your brother in the day of his misfortune; you should not have rejoiced over the people of Judah in the day of their ruin; you should not have boasted in the day of distress.

You should not have entered the gate of my people in the day of their calamity; you should not have gloated over his disaster in the day of his calamity; you should not have laid hands on his wealth in the day of his calamity.
You should not have stood at the crossroads to cut off his fugitives; you should not have handed over his survivors in the day of distress.
For the Day of YHWH (Yom YHWH) is near upon all the nations. As you have done, it shall be done to you; your dealing shall return upon your own head.
verses.note: THEOLOGICAL: "Yom YHWH" (the Day of YHWH) is a major eschatological motif across the Twelve Prophets (Joel 1:15; Amos 5:18-20; Zeph 1:14-18; Mal 4:5). In Obadiah it functions as the hinge of the entire oracle — particular judgment on Edom expands to universal judgment on all nations. The lex talionis principle ("as you have done, it shall be done to you") grounds divine judgment in moral symmetry. TYPOLOGY: The Day of YHWH finds its ultimate fulfillment in the return of the Messiah, when all nations face the divine tribunal (Rev 19:11-21; Matt 25:31-46).

words.g: a mountain or range of hills

words.m: conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 3rd common plur
conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 3rd common plur
conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 3rd common plur

verses.flow: For as you have drunk on my holy mountain, so all the nations shall drink continually; they shall drink and swallow, and shall be as though they had never been.

words.g: and + in/with a mountain or range of hills

words.m: conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 3rd masc sing
conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 3rd common plur

verses.flow: But on Mount Zion there shall be those who escape (pelitah), and it shall be holy; and the house of Jacob shall possess their own possessions.
verses.note: HEBREW: "Pelitah" (H6413, "escape/deliverance/remnant that has escaped") establishes the remnant theology central to OT soteriology. Mount Zion as the locus of deliverance connects to the broader Zion theology of the Psalms and Isaiah (Ps 48; Isa 46:13). TYPOLOGY: The escaped remnant on holy Zion anticipates both the post-exilic restoration and, ultimately, the messianic community gathered to the heavenly Zion (Heb 12:22; Rev 14:1, where the 144,000 stand with the Lamb on Mount Zion).

words.m: conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 3rd masc sing
conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 3rd common plur
conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 3rd common plur + suffix

verses.flow: The house of Jacob shall be a fire, and the house of Joseph a flame, and the house of Esau stubble; they shall burn them and consume them, and there shall be no survivor for the house of Esau, for YHWH has spoken.
verses.note: THEOLOGICAL: The imagery of Jacob/Joseph as fire and Esau as stubble deliberately reverses the cultural expectation — the numerically smaller and weaker nation becomes the instrument of divine consuming judgment upon the prouder. The unconditional "YHWH has spoken" (ki YHWH dibber) closes the oracle with the divine seal of certainty. TYPOLOGY: The consuming fire of divine judgment mediated through covenant Israel points forward to the Messiah's role as eschatological judge (Mal 3:1-3; Luke 12:49).

words.m: conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 3rd common plur

words.g: a mountain or range of hills

words.m: conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 3rd common plur

verses.flow: Those of the Negeb shall possess the mount of Esau, and those of the Shephelah shall possess the land of the Philistines; they shall possess the territory of Ephraim and the territory of Samaria, and Benjamin shall possess Gilead.

words.g: in/with a city in the widest sense

verses.flow: The exiles of this host of the people of Israel shall possess the land of the Canaanites as far as Zarephath, and the exiles of Jerusalem who are in Sepharad shall possess the cities of the Negeb.

words.m: conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 3rd common plur

a mountain or range of hills

words.m: conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 3rd fem sing

verses.flow: Deliverers (moshi'im) shall go up on Mount Zion to rule Mount Esau, and the kingdom (mamlakhah) shall be YHWH's.
verses.note: HEBREW: "Moshi'im" (H3467 root, yasha, "to save/deliver") — translated "deliverers/saviors," the same root from which "Yeshua" (Jesus) is derived. These deliverers ascending Zion to govern Mount Esau anticipate the messianic judges of the restored kingdom. "Mamlakhah" (H4467, "kingdom/dominion/reign") — the climactic declaration that sovereignty belongs to YHWH alone. TYPOLOGY: The kingdom being YHWH's finds direct fulfillment in the Messiah's eternal reign (Dan 7:14; Rev 11:15, "The kingdom of the world has become the kingdom of our Lord and of his Messiah"). DIVINE_COUNCIL: The establishment of YHWH's singular mamlakhah signals the final dissolution of the illegitimate authority of the divine council's rebellious sons (Ps 82; Deut 32:8-9), as all nations are reclaimed under YHWH's direct sovereign governance through the Messiah.
